title: Spectral analysis
abstract:
Abstract
               In this chapter, we re-visit and further develop the so-called frequency-domain approach to time-series analysis, whereby the stochastic variation in a time series of length n is modelled through a decomposition into harmonic components (sine-cosine waves) at the Fourier frequencies, fk=2πk/n:k=1,…,n/2. We first describe how the estimation of the spectrum from a single time series is improved by smoothing the periodogram to achieve a better trade-off between bias and variance. We then consider ways of comparing estimates of the spectrum from two or more time series, leading to a representation of a set of periodograms as a generalized linear model.
